flashcards.front: What is a Relation in the relational model?
flashcards.back: A Relation is a two-dimensional table with rows (tuples) and columns (attributes). It has a unique name and contains atomic (indivisible) values.
A Tuple is a single row in a relation, representing a record of data. It is an ordered set of attribute values.
An Attribute is a named column in a relation that represents a specific property of an entity. Each attribute has a domain (set of permissible values).
Domain is the set of all possible values that an attribute can take. It defines the type and constraints for attribute values (e.g., integers, strings, dates).

flashcards.front: What is the Degree of a relation?
flashcards.back: Degree (or Arity) is the number of attributes (columns) in a relation. A relation with n attributes is called an n-ary relation.
Cardinality is the number of tuples (rows) in a relation at a given time. It changes as records are inserted or deleted.
A Super Key is a set of one or more attributes that uniquely identifies a tuple in a relation. It can have redundant attributes.
A Candidate Key is a minimal super key (no proper subset is a key) that uniquely identifies tuples. A relation can have multiple candidate keys.
The Primary Key is the selected candidate key used to uniquely identify tuples in a relation. It cannot contain NULL values and must have unique values.
An Alternate Key is any candidate key that is not chosen as the Primary Key. It can also uniquely identify tuples but is not the primary identifier.
A Foreign Key is an attribute(s) in a relation that references the Primary Key of another relation (or the same relation). It establishes relationships between tables.
A Composite Key is a candidate key consisting of two or more attributes. The combination must be unique, though individual attributes may not be unique.
Entity Integrity states that the Primary Key cannot have NULL values. Every tuple must have a valid, unique identifier.
Referential Integrity ensures that foreign key values either match a Primary Key value in the referenced relation or are NULL.

flashcards.front: What is a NULL value in databases?
flashcards.back: NULL represents missing or unknown information. It is not zero or empty string. It means the value is either unknown, not applicable, or not yet assigned.
